arts.ttl: Potential Boro team for Millwall with telling Carrick comment and Howson call
arts.sum: Carrick’s main decisions centre around whether to keep Jonny Howson at centre-back now Rav van den Berg and Dael Fry are both fit again, and also whether to persist with Kelechi Iheanacho after he was booed by his own fans on Tuesday. There’s an argument that, given how Millwall likely play, the game is suited to Fry and Van den Berg.

arts.ttl: Middlesbrough academy challenge as Carrick issues 'you need that moment' message
arts.sum: Michael Carrick insists there will always be a pathway for young players to break into the first team at Middlesbrough, but says numerous factors will determine when the right time is. Carrick has shown great pleasure in handing numerous opportunities to Boro academy players - with numerous debuts handed out in his two-and-a-half-year stay to date.

arts.ttl: Van den Berg starts again: Middlesbrough team news and predicted XI to face Millwall
arts.sum: The latest Middlesbrough team news as Michael Carrick’s side gear up to face Millwall in the Championship. The post Van den Berg starts again: Middlesbrough team news and predicted XI to face Millwall appeared first on The72 - Football League News.

arts.ttl: Michael Carrick's Middlesbrough play-off race expectation and managing emotions
arts.sum: And while such twists are likely to make the race for the play-offs exciting for us as spectators, Carrick insists he and his players can’t get too emotionally involved. There may be only five games remaining but Michael Carrick is expecting many twists and turns yet in the race for the Championship top six.

arts.ttl: Casper De Norre hoping Millwall’s play-off push gathers momentum
arts.sum: Asked about how big an achievement it would be to finish in the play-off zone, Belgian midfielder De Norre said: “It would definitely mean a lot because if you are realistic, for Millwall, I don’t think you can get top two. “It was a really good performance at Sheffield United and you could really feel it after the game that it is a real confidence booster going forward, a little more belief that we are really in it now.

arts.ttl: Kelechi Iheanacho 'fuss' dismissed as Michael Carrick discusses his Middlesbrough role
arts.sum: Asked once more about the reaction Iheanacho received, and the subsequent talk around his continued place in the team, the Boro boss once again defended the Nigerian international, pointing to his contributions in the two games prior to the Leeds loss. One of the talking points around Iheanacho is the role he’s played for Boro so far.

arts.ttl: Aidan Morris on 'tense' Middlesbrough play-off chase, Michael Carrick importance and England move
arts.sum: Aidan Morris says he and the Middlesbrough players are embracing the ‘tough’ and ‘tense’ play-off race, insisting they believe in themselves to achieve their goals this season. “I just try to learn as much as I can from him,” Morris said.

arts.ttl: Edmundson, Lenihan, Bangura - Middlesbrough injury update and the Jonny Howson decision - Teesside Live
arts.sum: With Fry and Van den Berg now ready to start games again, it gives Carrick a dilemma heading to Millwall. Middlesbrough won’t welcome any new players back for the weekend’s trip to Millwall, with Boro still waiting on George Edmundson returning to training.

arts.ttl: Championship: Predictions for all 12 weekend games
arts.sum: The finishing line is coming into view in the Championship but there remains plenty to play for as Aaron Ashley previews all 12 games in the second tier this weekend.
